messages.content: You are an expert residential real estate appraiser for Cresci Appraisal & Consulting Company (CACC). You write USPAP-compliant appraisal reports in a professional, concise, data-driven style. You reference specific comparables by number, include market conditions context, and every sentence adds value.

Write the "comments on sales comparison" narrative section for this appraisal report.

Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 201 N Jayne St, Heyworth, IL
Year Built: 1979
Appraised Value: $170,000

Due to a lack of recent comparable sales in Heyworth, IL, comparables from competing communities have been included to demonstrate the marketability of the subject. The comparables have not been applied with a market time adjustment due to stability of sale prices in the market area. Comparable #1 has been adjusted for its location on a busy road, site size, bathroom count, and lack of an enclosed porch. Comparable #2 has been adjusted for its site size, age, brick exterior, superior condition, bathroom count, unfinished basement, lack of a garage, and lack of an enclosed porch. Comparable #3 has been adjusted for its site size, view, brick exterior, GLA, lack of an enclosed porch, and lack of a fence. Comparable #4 has been adjusted for its site size, age, condition due to recent remodeling, bathroom count, bedroom count, GLA, lack of a garage, lack of an enclosed porch, lack of a fireplace and fence. Comparable #5 has been adjusted for its site size, quality of construction, age, bathroom count, GLA, lack of an enclosed porch, and lack of a fireplace. Comparable #6 is an active listing, but has been included due to its proximity to the subject and similar condition, quality of construction, and enclosed porch. It has been adjusted for its site size, age, garage space count, lack of a fireplace, and lack of a fence. Comparable #7 has been included to further demonstrate the marketability of houses with a similar design, condition, and quality to the subject. It has been adjusted for its location, site size, GLA, lack of an enclosed porch, and lack of a fireplace. Comparable #8 is an older sale, but has been included to bracket the subject's sale price and has been adjusted for its site size, age, bathroom count, GLA, unfinished basement area, lack of an enclosed porch, lack of a fireplace, and lack of a fenced-in yard. A search was made for comparable properties to bracket the subject's site size, but none were found. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.

When summarizing adjustments, reference net and gross percentages. FNMA guidelines suggest net adjustments not exceed 15% and gross adjustments not exceed 25% of comparable sale price.

Calculate adjustments for Comparable Sale #5:

SUBJECT: N/A | ? SF | Built 2001 | C3
COMP #5: Sale Price $262,500 | ? SF (approx)

Provide the net and gross adjustment summary.

After analyzing the differences between the subject and Comparable #5, the following net adjustment applies:

Net Adjustment: +$10,100 (-3.8% net / 14.4% gross)
Adjusted Sale Price: $252,400

The 14.4% gross adjustment is within acceptable guidelines. The comparable required upward adjustment primarily for differences in site, condition, and/or GLA relative to the subject property. The adjusted price provides a reliable indicator of market value.

Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 112 Marsh Hawk Dr, Le Roy, IL
Year Built: 2004
Appraised Value: $330,000

Highest and Best Use criteria: The reasonably probable and legal use of vacant land or an improved property that is physically possible, appropriately supported, financially feasible, and that results in the highest value. The four criteria the highest and best use must meet are legal permissibility, physical possibility, financial feasibility, and maximum productivity.  Underwriter Conditions  "please add seller credit 1000"  The sales concession of $1,000 in the form of a closing cost credit has been added to the appraisal report.   Appraiser to correct seller credit on page 1. Credit for carpet allowance is no longer applicable. Only a $1000 credit for closing costs will be given by sellers.  The sales concession has been corrected to only reflect a $1,000 credit for closing costs.  Our end investor has asked that the following be addressed/corrected: 1. Please correct the borrowers names to Brian D. Murphy and Taylor A. Hamilton.  The borrowers' names have been updated to Brian D. Murphy and Taylor A. Hamilton.  2. The photos indicate a Great Room and the building sketch indicates a living room. Please explain the discrepancy  The Great Room's label on the sketch has been updated to reflect its label on the picture page.

Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 313 Hillside Ln, Bloomington, IL
Year Built: 1969
Appraised Value: $275,000

All comparables are found in close proximity to the subject property or in competing neighborhoods in Bloomington-Normal, IL. The subject's neighborhood is located near the border of Bloomington and Normal. The comparables located in Normal still represent an accurate demonstration of marketability due to the subject and comparables sharing the same market area. The comparables were applied with a market time adjustment of 5.8% based on the sales statistics chart included in this report. Comparable #1 has been adjusted for its site size, inferior condition, bathroom count, walkout basement, basement finished room count, and two fireplaces. Comparable #2 has been adjusted for its site size, and walkout basement. Comparable #3 has been adjusted for its site size, superior condition due to recent updates, GLA, basement finished room count, fireplace count. Comparable #4 has been adjusted for its site size, GLA, basement finished room count, lack of a deck, and fireplace count. Comparable #5 has been adjusted for its GLA, basement finished room count, covered patio, and fireplace count. Comparable #6 is located in close proximity to the subject property and has been included to further demonstrate the marketability of houses with a similar quality, conditon, and basement finish. It has been adjusted for its sales concessions, site size, bathroom count, GLA, basement finished room count, and enclosed porch. The adjustments applied to comparables' basements took into consideration condition, room count, and inclusion of a wet bar. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.

$286,000

Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 25316 E 1100 North Rd, Downs, IL
Year Built: 1978
Appraised Value: $465,000

Due to a lack of recent comparable sales in the subject's immediate market area, an extensive search was made to find recent comparable sales in competing rural areas that would appeal to a similar market segment as the subject. The comparables were selected to demonstrate the marketability of houses of a similar location, site size, quality, and condition. Due to recent market trends the comparables have received a market time adjustment of 4.2% based on the sales statistics chart included in this report. Comparable #1 has been adjusted for its condition, bathroom count, GLA, basement finished room count, garage space count, lack of an enclosed porch, fireplace count, outbuilding, and accessory dwelling unit. The sqft, quality, and condition of the ADU of Comparable #1 was considered and a significant adjustment was made to account for its contributory value. However, an ADU is atypical for properties in a rural location and did not appear to have a significant impact on the marketability of Comparable #1. Comparable #2 has been adjusted for its sales concessions, site size, quality, age, bathroom count, GLA, lack of a basement, heating/cooling system, lack of an enclosed porch, lack of a fireplace, and outbuilding. Comparable #3 has been adjusted for its location, view, bathroom count, GLA, walkout basement, heating/cooling system, garage space count, patio, and fireplace count. Comparable #4 has been adjusted for its sales concessions, site size, quality, age, bathroom count, GLA, lack of a finished basement, heating/cooling system, garage space count, lack of an enclosed porch, fireplace count, and shed. Comparable #5 has been adjusted for its condition, bathroom count, GLA, walkout basement, basement finished room count, heating/cooling system, garage space count, patio, and fireplace count. Comparable #6 has been adjusted for its age, bathroom count, GLA, basement finished room count, heating/cooling system, garage space count, fireplace count, and quonset shed. Comparable #7 has been included to bracket the subject's age. It has been adjusted for its site size, age, bathroom count, GLA, lack of a main floor bedroom, heating/cooling system, garage space count, fireplace count, outbuilding, and fence. Comparable #7's reported GLA on public record does not include the finished attic area currently utilized as a bedroom. 301 Ireland Grove Rd, Bloomington, IL  was considered to bracket the subject's above grade bathroom count, but was deemed to be an uncomparable recent sale. An extenstive search was made to find a recent comparable sale to bracket the subject's GLA, but none were identified. Based on the comparables' sale price per gross living area, it appears that the subject sold below market value. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.   Underwriter Conditions  Appraiser states that the interior of the 2 detached garages were not accessible at time of inspection. Seller must grant appraiser access to the garages and appraiser to update the appraisal accordingly.  -Seller granted access to the exterior garages and photos of their interiors have been included in this report.

Section 1455.245  Scope of Property Condition Inspections by Real Estate Appraisers   Licensed real estate appraisers may include in an appraisal report comments on the condition of the property that affect property value, including physical deficiencies, adverse conditions and renovations, based on observations by the licensed real estate appraiser during the appraisal of the property. Licensed real estate appraisers may not provide these comments for compensation, or with the expectation of receiving compensation, directly or indirectly, as a substitute for a home inspection, or a home inspection report, prepared by a licensed home inspector. All appraisal reports of a property that is 1 to 4 residential units prepared by licensed real estate appraisers must include the following statement: "The comments by the licensed real estate appraiser contained within this appraisal report on the condition of the property do not address "standards of practice" as defined in the Home Inspector License Act [225 ILCS 441] and 68 Ill. Adm. Code 1410 and are not to be considered a home inspection or home inspection report." Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 306 Loma Dr, Forsyth, IL
Year Built: 1974
Appraised Value: $257,000

All comparables have been found in either close proximity to the subject property or in competing neighborhoods in Forsyth, IL. The comparables were selected to demonstrate the marketability of houses with a similar design, age, condition, quality, and room count to the subject. Due to recent market trends in Forsyth, IL a market time adjustment of 4% was applied to the comparables based on the sales statistics chart included in this report. Comparable #1 has been adjusted for its site size, condition due to recent updates, GLA, and basement finished room count. Comparables #2 & #3 are located in a competing neighborhood, but have been included because they demonstrate the marketability of houses in the market area with a similar design, age, quality, condition, and room count. Comparable #2 has been adjusted for its site size, walkout basement, basement finished room count, garage space count, deck size, and fenced-in yard. Comparable #3 has been adjusted for its site size, walkout basement, and fireplace count. A search was made for comparable properties in the market area with a similar design, age, condition, and quality to bracket the subject's contract price, but none were found. Based on the comparables' sale price per GLA, it appears that the subject has sold  below market value and that the market is willing to pay at least $175/sqft for a house of a similar design, quality, and condition. Due to the limited adjustments made most weight has been placed on Comparables #1 & #3. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.   Underwriter Conditions  Appraiser to correct placing of the legal description.  - The legal description has been reformatted to fix placing errors during file conversion. Sorry for the inconvenience.   Appraiser to address SSR findings- Comp #2- The reported total basement area is materially different than what has been reported by other appraisers please confirm report is accurate.  - Comparable #2 reported total basement area is supported by the building characteristics information found in public record and cross referenced with information avaliable on the MLS listing #11878419. The total basement area reported was deemed to be accurate based on the information avaliable to the appraiser at the time of the report.   Comp #3- The quality rating is materially different than what has been reported by other appraisers, please confirm report is accurate.  - Comparable #3 quality rating was based on the dwelling's partial brick construction and interior finish. This was deemed to most accurately fit the Q3 definition of "Dwellings with this quality rating are residences of higher quality built from individual or readily available designer plans in above-standard residential tract developments or on an individual property owner's site. The design includes significant exterior ornamentation and interiors that are well finished. The workmanship exceeds acceptable standards and many materials and finishes throughout the dwelling have been upgraded from "stock" standards." The Q3 rating based on these characteristics of the property is consistent with the other reported comparables and subject quality ratings.

Highest and Best Use criteria: The reasonably probable and legal use of vacant land or an improved property that is physically possible, appropriately supported, financially feasible, and that results in the highest value. The four criteria the highest and best use must meet are legal permissibility, physical possibility, financial feasibility, and maximum productivity.  Extra Comments ANSI Standards Gross living area calculations are derived from the measurement of the subject property and reported on the dimension and sketch pages of this report. Depreciation is estimated by the appraiser observations. The appraiser has adhered to ANSI standards in measuring the subject property.  Clarification of Intended Use and Intended User: First State Mortgage The Intended User of this appraisal report is the Lender/Client. The Intended Use is to evaluate the property that is the subject of this appraisal for a mortgage finance transaction, subject to the stated Scope of Work, purpose of the appraisal, reporting requirements of this appraisal report form, and Definition of Market Value. No additional Intended Users are identified by the appraiser.  Effective January 1st, 2010, the ETHICS RULE now requires an appraiser to disclose any prior services provided regarding the subject property within the three years preceding the new request / assignment. The appraiser has not appraised the property in the past three years preceeding this request.   This appraisal report adheres to the development and reporting options of The Uniform Standards of Professional Appraisal Practice (USPAP), FIRREA Title XI Appraisal Regulations and The Appraisal Independence Standards set forth by H.R 4173.  Geographical Competency Statement: This appraiser has geographic competency. The appraiser has prepared the appraisal in a familiar location and has spent sufficient time to understand the nuances of the local market and the supply and demand factors relating to the specific property type and the location involved.  Section 1455.245  Scope of Property Condition Inspections by Real Estate Appraisers   Licensed real estate appraisers may include in an appraisal report comments on the condition of the property that affect property value, including physical deficiencies, adverse conditions and renovations, based on observations by the licensed real estate appraiser during the appraisal of the property. Licensed real estate appraisers may not provide these comments for compensation, or with the expectation of receiving compensation, directly or indirectly, as a substitute for a home inspection, or a home inspection report, prepared by a licensed home inspector. All appraisal reports of a property that is 1 to 4 residential units prepared by licensed real estate appraisers must include the following statement: "The comments by the licensed real estate appraiser contained within this appraisal report on the condition of the property do not address "standards of practice" as defined in the Home Inspector License Act [225 ILCS 441] and 68 Ill. Adm. Code 1410 and are not to be considered a home inspection or home inspection report." Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 6003 Mclean Rd, Stanford, IL
Year Built: 1927
Appraised Value: $255,000

All comparables have been found in competing rural communities in Central Illinois. Due to recent market trends, all comparables have been applied with a 7.3% market time adjusted based on the sales statistics included in this report. All comparables were selected for their similar acreage and ammenties to the subject including barns with stables and other outbuildings. A search was made for comparable properties to bracket the subject's site size, but none were found. Comparable #1 has been adjusted for its site size, age, condition, bathroom count, basement room count, garage space count, lack of an enclosed porch, large shed, and lack of a chicken coop. Comparable #2 has been adjusted for its site size, age, bathroom count, GLA, lack of a generator, lack of an enclosed porch and deck. Comparable #3 has been adjusted for its site size, age, condition due to recent remodeling, bathroom count, GLA, lack of a basement, lack of a generator, lack of an enclosed porch, and fireplace. Due to a lack of recent comparable sales, Comparables #4 and #5 are active listings and have been included to further demonstrate the marketability of rural properties with similar acreage and ammenities. Comparable #4 has been adjusted for its residential location, site size, age, condition, walkout basment, lack of a basement kitchen, lack of outbuilding stables, lack of a shed, and fireplace. Comparable #5 has been adjusted for its site size, condition, bathroom count, GLA, basement room count, lack of a generator, lack of an enclosed porch, horse barn size, shed count, fireplace, and fence. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.   Conditions on Appraisal  "appraiser to address conditions on FNMA SSR due to 4.3 score"  The 4.3 score reflects the lack of recent comparable sales near the subject property, primarily due to its rural location. The selected comparable properties share similar amenities, quality, and condition with the subject property. After making the necessary adjustments, these comparable properties provide an accurate estimate of the property's value.

Form: FNM1004
Purpose: Purchase
Address: 2007 E Jackson St, Bloomington, IL
Year Built: 1920
Appraised Value: $285,000

All comparables have been found in close proximity to the subject property in Bloomington, IL. The comparables were selected to demonstrate the marketability of houses in the Founder's Grove neighborhood of Bloomington, IL with similar GLA, condition, and amenities to the subject. A search was made for comparable properties to bracket the subject's site size, but due to the subject's larger site size than most properties in the market area none were found. Comparable #1 has been adjusted for its site size, brick construction, bathroom count, GLA, main floor bedroom, garage space count, lack of an enclosed porch, and lack of whirlpool tub. Comparable #1 features a finished detatched studio that was used by the previous owner as an art studio. This aspect of the comparable was not given an adjustment as the utilitity is similar in value to that of the large detatched garage/workshop in the subject's backyard. Comparable #2 has been adjusted for its site size, brick construction, bathroom count, GLA, basement bathroom, garage space count, lack of a whirpool tub, lack of a detatched workshop, and fenced-in yard. Comparable #3 has been adjusted for its site size, bathroom count, GLA, basement bathroom, main floor bedroom, garage space count, enclosed porch count, lack of a whirlpool tub, lack of a detatched workshop, and fenced-in yard. Comparable #4 has been included to further demonstrate the marketability of houses of a similar condition and GLA in Founder's Grove. It has been adjusted for its site size, brick construction, bathroom count, basment finished room count, garage space count, and lack of a detatched workshop. Comparable #5 has been included to bracket the subject's GLA. Comparable #5 has been adjusted for its site size, bathroom count, GLA, finished attic, garage space count, lack of a detatched workshop, and fenced-in yard. Although it is an older sale, it has not been given a market time adjustment due to stability of sale prices in the market area. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.

Comparable #3 is located in the competing rural community of Lexington, IL. Comparable #4 is found in the competing rural community of Ellsworth, IL. Comparable #5 is found in the competing rural community of Hudson, IL and demonstrates the marketability of houses on a similar site size. Comparable #6 is an active listing and has been included to further demonstrate the marketability of rural properties in McLean County. It has been adjusted for its site size, lack of a view of a retention pond, age, bathrom count, condition, GLA, basement size and finished room count, garage space count, and fireplaces. A search was made for comparables of a similar style, quality, and condition to bracket the subject's site size, but none were found. After adjustments are made the comparables provide a good basis for an estimate of value.
